topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is a collection of various materials and tools used by System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers.

entries.q: Who is the Book of Secret Knowledge repository aimed at?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is aimed at System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers, but it can be useful for anyone.

entries.q: How can I contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: You can contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository by making a pull request, adding valid and well-reasoned explanations about your changes or comments, and following the contributing guidelines.

entries.q: What type of content does the Book of Secret Knowledge repository contain?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository contains a collection of inspiring lists, manuals, cheatsheets, blogs, hacks, one-liners, cli/web tools, and more.

entries.q: What are some examples of CLI tools included in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Some examples of CLI tools included in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository are GNU Bash, Zsh, tclsh, bash-it, and Oh My ZSH!

entries.q: How can I stay up-to-date with changes to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: You can stay up-to-date with changes to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository by using the RSS/Atom feed of the commits.

entries.q: What are some of the main chapters in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Some of the main chapters in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository include CLI Tools, GUI Tools, Web Tools, Systems/Services, Networks, and more.

entries.q: What is the license of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is licensed under the MIT License.
